relatedness_reasoning: Pre-approved credit offers from marketing flow into the credit department, which then converts them into formal loan applications for risk and compliance review. The inbound file provides customer, offer, and eligibility data that naturally transforms into a loan application structure for downstream compliance assessment.

mappings.reasoning: Offer ID from marketing becomes the application ID in the credit system; both uniquely identify a credit product instance.
Exact field match; both record when the record was created/submitted.
Exact field match; both uniquely identify the customer.
Concatenation of first and last name from customer details.
No date of birth field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
Exact field match; both contain customer email address.
Exact field match; both contain customer phone number.
Inbound product_type is 'personal_loan' enum; outbound loan_type includes 'Personal Loan' as an enum value.
Offer amount becomes the requested loan amount; both represent the principal credit being offered.
Exact field match; both contain ISO 4217 currency code.
Exact field match; both specify loan repayment period in months.
Inbound provides interest_rate (decimal percentage) but not interest_rate_type (Fixed/Variable classification).
No loan purpose field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
Inbound employment_status is an eligibility requirement; outbound captures the applicant's actual employment status.
No employer name field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No job title field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
Inbound specifies minimum annual income required for eligibility; outbound captures actual applicant income, which may differ.
No employment start date field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No total assets field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No total liabilities field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
Inbound specifies minimum credit score required; outbound captures actual applicant credit score, which may exceed the minimum.
No existing loans count field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No default history field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No credit inquiries field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No collateral or property value field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No property location field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No loan-to-value ratio field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
Inbound status is 'pending_credit_review'; outbound status includes 'Under Review' and 'Pending Compliance Review' as the application progresses.
No submitted_by or credit officer identifier field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema.
No risk category field exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema; this is assigned by the credit department during processing.
No compliance checks specification exists in the inbound pre-approved offer schema; this is determined by credit department policy.
Inbound department_from is 'marketing'; outbound source_department is 'Credit', indicating the credit department is now the source for this outbound message.
Destination is 'Risk and Compliance', which is not specified in the inbound schema; this is determined by the credit system's routing logic.
No batch date field exists in the inbound schema; this is generated when the credit department batches applications for submission.
No batch ID field exists in the inbound schema; this is system-generated by the credit department.
No total applications count exists in the inbound schema; this is computed from the batch size.
No data classification field exists in the inbound schema; this is assigned by the credit system's security policy.
